name: division_with_oversized_concat_divisor
description: Code should include division where the divisor is a concatenation of a single-bit constant and a wider signal, producing a value that exceeds the declared bit width of the divisor operand, causing ctlz() to return an anomalous value and triggering an assertion failure in udivmod.

name: unsigned_division_operand_width_mismatch
description: Code should include unsigned division where an operand's value exceeds its declared bit width, causing ctlz() to produce invalid results that lead to division algorithm failures in CXXRTL.
snippet: wire [6:0] divisor = {1'b1, wide_signal}; wire [6:0] result = dividend / divisor;

name: division_with_value_exceeding_bit_width
description: Code should include division where the actual value of an operand (after assignment or concatenation) exceeds what can be represented in its declared bit width, leading to runtime assertion failures in the division routine.
snippet: input wire in2; wire [6:0] divisor = {1'b1, in2}; assign out = 7'b11100 / divisor;
